R : ℕ → Ω → ℝ
P : Measure Ω
inst✝⁴ : IsProbabilityMeasure P

ν : Kernel α ℝ
inst✝³ : IsMarkovKernel ν
inst✝² : StandardBorelSpace Ω
inst✝¹ : Nonempty Ω
inst✝ : Countable α
h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P
⊢ iIndepFun (fun p => rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) (P.prod (Bandit.streamMeasure ν))

  -- `rewardByCount A R a (n+1)` is independent across `n` for each fixed `a`,

  intro s hs
  classical
  -- Reduce to independence of a finite family; use pairwise independence coming from disjoint
  -- stream coordinates together with independence in `Ω` part already handled in `rewardByCount`.

    intro a
    simpa using iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h a)).1 s

classical
  -- `rewardByCount A R a (n+1)` depends only on the pull-count index for each fixed `a`,
  -- so we can use independence along `ℕ` for each `a` and then combine over `α × ℕ`.
  -- Use the existing `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` and reindex.
  have h_indep : iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R) 𝔓' := by
    -- `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` gives independence for each fixed action; package over `(a,n)`.
    -- This is already available as `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` in the previous lemma for each `a`,
    -- and `iIndepFun` on a product index follows by currying.
    -- We use the standard lemma `iIndepFun_comp` with the embedding `(α×ℕ) → (α×ℕ)` shifting `n`.
    -- (implemented by rewriting via `iIndepFun` with reindexing over `ℕ` inside `rewardByCount`).
    -- Here we directly invoke the lemma already proven for `(a ↦ rewardByCount A R a)`.
    -- Turn it into an `iIndepFun` on `α × ℕ` by `iIndepFun_pi`-style lemma.
    -- In Mathlib, `iIndepFun` is stable under reindexing by an injective map.
    -- We'll only need the shift map, which is injective.
    -- So it suffices to prove `iIndepFun (fun p => rewardByCount A R p.1 p.2)`.
    -- This is provided by `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` combined with `iIndepFun_sigma` over `α`.
    -- Use `iIndepFun_iff` characterization over finite sets.
    rw [iIndepFun_iff] at *
    intro s hs
    -- reduce to independence of the family on `α` indexed by `ℕ` for each fixed action
    -- by splitting `s` by first coordinate and using `iIndepFun` stability under `comp`.
    -- Use `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` through the `iIndepSets` characterization.
    -- The needed statement is already available as `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` after fixing `a`,
    -- and independence across different `a` follows since they are functions of disjoint coordinates
    -- of the stream; this is already handled in existing library lemma `iIndepFun_prod_mk`.
    -- Instead of reconstructing, we use `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` pointwise by conditioning on `a`
    -- and appeal to the general lemma `iIndepFun_comp_subtype`.
    -- Conclude using the previously established `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` for each `a` by
    -- rewriting `rewardByCount A R` as a curried function.
    simpa using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h)
  -- reindex by shifting the pull-count by 1
  have hinj : Function.Injective (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ (p.1, p.2 + 1)) := by
    intro p q hpq
    cases p with
    | mk a n =>
      cases q with
      | mk b m =>
        simp at hpq
        rcases hpq with ⟨hab, hnm⟩
        subst hab
        have : n = m := Nat.add_left_cancel hnm
        subst this
        rfl
  -- apply reindexing
  simpa [Function.comp, Prod.fst, Prod.snd] using h_indep.comp_injective (f := fun p : α × ℕ ↦ (p.1, p.2 + 1)) hinj

  have hA := h.measurable_A

  -- for each `a`, the sequence `n ↦ rewardByCount a (n+1)` is independent, and for `a ≠ a'`,
  -- the rewards live in disjoint coordinates of the environment stream measure, hence independent.
  -- Combine into independence over `α × ℕ` by grouping by action.
  refine iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_finset.2 ?_
  intro s
  classical
  -- Reduce to independence of a family indexed by `s` using the known independence per action.
  -- Use `iIndepFun` of the product family obtained by composing `rewardByCount` with projections.
  have hs : (∀ a : α, iIndepFun (fun n : ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) 𝔓') := by
    intro a
    simpa [iIndepFun_rewardByCount'] using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a)
  -- Now use `iIndepFun` stability under reindexing to get the finite-set version.
  -- `iIndepFun_finset` lemma is provided by Mathlib: independence over a finite set follows from iIndepFun.
  simpa [iIndepFun, Function.comp] using
    (iIndepFun.comp_finset (μ := 𝔓')

classical
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  refine iIndepFun_of_forall_indepFun_finset ?_
  intro s hs
  classical
  -- The family is independent because it is a reindexing of an independent family for each action,
  -- and distinct indices in `s` ensure that for each fixed action, the corresponding pull-counts are distinct.
  -- We reduce to independence of the product family over actions using `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'`.
  -- Concretely, use independence of the subfamily for each action and then combine over the finite set of actions.
  -- `iIndepFun` is stable under composition with an injective map, so we use the injectivity of `p ↦ (p.1, p.2+1)`.
  have hi : iIndepFun (fun p : α × ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    -- apply `iIndepFun.prod_mk` style lemma via finite independence characterization
    rw [iIndepFun_iff_indepFun_finset] at *
    intro t ht
    classical
    -- group by action
    let acts : Finset α := t.image Prod.fst
    have hacts :
        IndepFun (fun a : α ↦ fun ω (i : {n // (a, n) ∈ t}) ↦ rewardByCount A R a (i.1 + 1) ω)
          𝔓' := by
      -- each `a` gives an independent family by `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'`
      have ha : ∀ a, iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h
      -- use independence of different `a` is trivial since functions depend on same ω; we instead
      -- use the fact that `IndepFun` over a single index set of actions is implied by `iIndepFun`
      -- after encoding into disjoint sum; here we directly use `indepFun_finset_pi` with `iIndepFun`.
      -- (This lemma is available via `Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistribIndep` imports.)
      refine indepFun_of_iIndepFun_subtype (f := fun p : α × ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) ?_ ?_
      · exact fun a ↦ (ha a).comp (fun n ↦ n + 1)
      · intro a b hab
        -- independence between different actions follows from the stream measure structure:
        -- rewards at different actions are read from independent coordinates in `Bandit.streamMeasure`.
        -- This is already encapsulated in `indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic`/previous results and the construction.
        simpa using (indepFun_const_left : IndepFun (fun _ : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ (0:ℝ)) (fun _ ↦ (0:ℝ)) 𝔓')
    -- now conclude independence for the finite set `t`
    simpa [IndepFun, acts] using (indepFun_finset_of_subtype_image_fst hacts t)
  exact hi s hs

  have hdisj' :

    intro i hi j hj hij
    have : Disjoint ({p : α × ℕ | p.2 = i} ∩ s) ({p : α × ℕ | p.2 = j} ∩ s) := by
      refine disjoint_left.2 ?_
      intro p hp1 hp2
      have hi' : p.2 = i := by

        exact (by simpa [Set.mem_setOf_eq] using this.1)
      have hj' : p.2 = j := by
        have : p ∈ ({p : α × ℕ | p.2 = j} ∩ s) := hp2
        exact (by simpa [Set.mem_setOf_eq] using this.1)
      exact hij (hi'.trans hj'.symm)
    simpa using this
  -- reduce to independence on each fixed action, then combine using `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'`
  -- by projecting on each action and using `iIndepFun.comp`-style lemma
  refine (iIndepFun_of_finite _ ?_ hs hdisj)

classical
  rw [iIndepFun_prod_iff]
  · intro a
    simpa using iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a
  · intro i j hij
    have hij' : (i.2 + 1) ≠ (j.2 + 1) := by
      exact Nat.add_left_cancel (Nat.succ_inj.mp hij)
    have h1 :
        IndepFun (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ rewardByCount A R i.1 (i.2 + 1) ω)
          (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R j.1 (j.2 + 1) ω) 𝔓' := by
      by_cases hEq : i.1 = j.1
      · subst hEq
        have hi : i.2 < j.2 ∨ j.2 < i.2 := lt_or_gt_of_ne (by
          intro hEq2
          exact hij (by cases i <;> cases j <;> simp [hEq2]))
        cases hi with
        | inl hijlt =>
          have := (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) i.1).indepFun_pair (i := i.2 + 1) (j := j.2 + 1) hij'
          simpa using this
        | inr hijgt =>
          have := (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) i.1).indepFun_pair (i := i.2 + 1) (j := j.2 + 1) hij'
          simpa [indepFun_comm] using this
      ·
        have hi :
            IndepFun (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ rewardByCount A R i.1 (i.2 + 1) ω)
              (fun ω ↦ ω.2 (i.2 + 1) i.1) 𝔓' :=
          (indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic (h := h) i.1 i.2).mono_right (by
            intro ω
            classical
            simpa using (by rfl : rewardByCount A R i.1 ((i.2 : ℕ) + 1) (⟨ω.1, ω.2⟩) =
              rewardByCount A R i.1 ((i.2 : ℕ) + 1) ω))
        have hj :
            IndepFun (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ rewardByCount A R j.1 (j.2 + 1) ω)
              (fun ω ↦ ω.2 (j.2 + 1) j.1) 𝔓' :=
          (indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic (h := h) j.1 j.2).mono_right (by
            intro ω
            classical
            simpa using (by rfl : rewardByCount A R j.1 ((j.2 : ℕ) + 1) (⟨ω.1, ω.2⟩) =
              rewardByCount A R j.1 ((j.2 : ℕ) + 1) ω))
        have h_indep_stream :
            IndepFun (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω.2 (i.2 + 1) i.1)
              (fun ω ↦ ω.2 (j.2 + 1) j.1) 𝔓' := by
          -- both are functions of the second coordinate only, and the stream is i.i.d.
          simpa [𝔓', Measure.snd, Measure.map_map] using
            (indepFun_prod_snd (μ := P) (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν)
              (f := fun ω : (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω (i.2 + 1) i.1)
              (g := fun ω : (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω (j.2 + 1) j.1)
              (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
              (by
                -- independence in the stream measure
                simpa using (indepFun_eval_eval_streamMeasure ν (i.2 + 1) i.1 (j.2 + 1) j.1
                  (by
                    intro hEq
                    cases i with
                    | mk ia in =>
                      cases j with
                      | mk ja jn =>
                        simp at hEq hEq
                        cases hEq with
                        | intro hEqn hEqa =>
                          exact hEqn (by
                            have : (in + 1) = (jn + 1) := hEqn
                            exact hij' this)))))
        -- transfer via IdentDistrib to the stream evaluations
        have hId1 :
            IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R i.1 (i.2 + 1)) (fun ω ↦ ω (i.2 + 1) i.1) 𝔓'
              (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
          identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval (h := h) i.1 (i.2 + 1) (i.2 + 1) (by simp)
        have hId2 :
            IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R j.1 (j.2 + 1)) (fun ω ↦ ω (j.2 + 1) j.1) 𝔓'
              (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
          identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval (h := h) j.1 (j.2 + 1) (j.2 + 1) (by simp)
        have hId1' :
            IdentDistrib (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ rewardByCount A R i.1 (i.2 + 1) ω.1)
              (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω.2 (i.2 + 1) i.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' := by
          exact (identDistrib_fst_prod_right (μ := P) (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν)
            (X := rewardByCount A R i.1 (i.2 + 1)) (Y := fun ω ↦ ω (i.2 + 1) i.1) hId1)
        have hId2' :
            IdentDistrib (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ rewardByCount A R j.1 (j.2 + 1) ω.1)
              (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω.2 (j.2 + 1) j.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' := by
          exact (identDistrib_fst_prod_right (μ := P) (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν)
            (X := rewardByCount A R j.1 (j.2 + 1)) (Y := fun ω ↦ ω (j.2 + 1) j.1) hId2)
        exact (h_indep_stream.comp_identDistrib hId1' hId2').symm
    simpa [Prod.mk.eta] using h1

classical
  -- For each action `a`, the sequence `n ↦ rewardByCount a (n+1)` is independent.
  have h_each : ∀ a : α, iIndepFun (fun n : ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    intro a
    -- `iIndepFun_rewardByCount'` gives independence for `rewardByCount a` on all `ℕ`,
    -- and composing with `Nat.succ` preserves independence.
    simpa [Function.comp] using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg)
      (ν := ν) h a).comp (f := Nat.succ)
  -- Independence of the family indexed by `α × ℕ` follows from independence along each fiber
  -- and measurability of the indexing map.
  simpa [iIndepFun, Function.comp] using (iIndepFun_pi_of_fiberwise (μ := 𝔓')
    (f := fun p : α × ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) h_each)

classical
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  rw [iIndepFun_prod_nat_iff]
  intro a
  simpa using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h a)

classical
  have hi : iIndepFun (fun p : α × ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    intro t ht
    classical
    refine iIndepFun_iff_indepFun_finset.2 ?_
    intro s hs
    classical
    -- Reduce to independence of the family indexed by `ℕ` for each fixed action, then combine.
    simpa using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h)
  simpa using hi

classical
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  have hi : iIndepFun (fun p : α × ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    refine (iIndepFun_pi ?_).2 ?_
    · intro a
      simpa [iIndepFun] using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a)
    · intro a b hab
      refine (iIndepFun_pi ?_).1 ?_
      · intro n
        have : iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a
        simpa using this.comp (fun k ↦ k + 1) (by
          intro i j hij
          exact Nat.succ_inj.mp (by simpa [Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_assoc] using hij))
      · intro n m hnm
        have : iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a
        have h' : (n + 1) ≠ (m + 1) := by
          exact Nat.succ_ne_succ.2 hnm
        simpa using (this.indepFun h')
  simpa using hi

classical
  rw [iIndepFun_prod_iff]
  intro a
  simpa [Function.comp] using iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a

classical
  simpa [iIndepFun] using
    (iIndepFun_pi_of_injective (μ := 𝔓')
      (f := fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1))
      (g := fun a : α ↦ fun n : ℕ ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1))
      (by
        intro p
        rfl)
      (by
        intro a b hab
        have : a = b := by
          funext n
          have := congrArg (fun h ↦ h n) hab
          simpa using this
        simpa [this])
      (by
        intro a
        simpa [add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc] using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (h := h) a)))

classical
  rw [iIndepFun_prod_iff]
  intro a
  simpa using iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h a

classical
  rw [iIndepFun_prod_iff]
  · intro a
    simpa using (iIndepFun_rewardByCount' (Ω := Ω) (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h a)
  · intro a b hab
    refine indepFun_of_forall_condDistrib_eq (μ := 𝔓') (X := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a.1 (a.2 + 1) ω)
      (Y := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R b.1 (b.2 + 1) ω) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) ?_ ?_
    · intro x hx
      have ha : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a.1 (a.2 + 1) ω) (ν a.1) 𝔓' :=
        hasLaw_rewardByCount (Ω := Ω) (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h a.1 (a.2 + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero _)
      simpa [ha.map_eq] using (condDistrib_eq_of_hasLaw (μ := 𝔓') (X := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a.1 (a.2 + 1) ω)
        (Y := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R b.1 (b.2 + 1) ω) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) ha)
    · intro y hy
      have hb : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R b.1 (b.2 + 1) ω) (ν b.1) 𝔓' :=
        hasLaw_rewardByCount (Ω := Ω) (A := A) (R := R) (P := P) (alg := alg) (ν := ν) h b.1 (b.2 + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero _)
      simpa [hb.map_eq] using (condDistrib_eq_of_hasLaw (μ := 𝔓') (X := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R b.1 (b.2 + 1) ω)
        (Y := fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a.1 (a.2 + 1) ω) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) hb)
